pages.full_text:
called Mitton, renamed by Brindley. But nowadays v. cheap fairground, incomprehensible language "... Bab!" v. poor [?pess] people. We bought lunch for £4.95 - a roast. Inedible chicken, teeth bounced as if chewing car tyre. Spat it out & have ready in doggy bag for Trader's supper. As bad as a meal near Garsford or the English lunch on the French canal.
	Austin (6) was BRILLIANT on the 15m like ride.
	The campsite used to be an American WW2 army hospital. Out of 10,000 patients only 4 died.
	Back to the camp for [?tea/teas] then set off home.

Oct 11th 2014 Saturday
	8.20. Simon, Eve & Tinder left for

Mandarin - stone, slate, granite for lights at Trumpington. Gorgeous stones for bathrooms & kitchens. Eve telephoned Kate & Bryony to tell them about it because they are putting in bathrooms & kitchens [illegible]
pages.notes: Top of page is a continuation from previous spread. 'Garsford' could also read 'Garsfield' or 'Garfield'. 'pess' is unclear — could be 'posh'. The text cuts off at the bottom of the left page mid-sentence. 'Tinder' appears to be a dog's name. 'Mandarin' refers to Mandarin Stone, a natural stone retailer; showroom at Trumpington.

pages.full_text:
at the farm. There is a showroom at Monmouth.
	Then on to Annette who took us out to lunch at Auberge. Big John's son is getting married. We had chicken/sausage (Eve) & Guinea fowl (Annette & Simon). Delicious. And crème brulée, raspberry sorbet & chocolate mousse.
	Then we went to tea with John & Pamela. Money shortage.
	On to __Kenninghall__ * * * * * South Lodge.
	Tiny. Shed for washroom. Good.
	Thunder, lightening, rain. The owner told us there'd be a concert of local music talent at the church at 7.30. We didn't really want to go out. But we did & it was super - David Knier played guitar & sang, John Loker played the mouth organ. They were terrific.
	The pub landlord also played the mouth organ. There is an arrangement that the
pages.notes: Page number 65 visible at top right. 'Kenninghall' is underlined and followed by asterisks — appears to be a campsite name or rating. 'South Lodge' is likely the campsite name at Kenninghall. 'Auberge' is a restaurant name. 'David Knier' — surname spelling uncertain, could be 'Knier' or 'Vinier'; the letter looks like a 'V' at start, so could read 'David Vinier'. 'lightening' is as written (non-standard spelling of 'lightning'). Text runs off the bottom of the page — continues on next spread.
